summary: Russia launched two Oreshik ballistic missiles on 24 May, striking Kyiv region and occupied Donetsk. Ukraine reports critical shortages in air defence and has initiated a logistical lockdown program targeting Russian rear assets. Numerous UAV incursions were observed across multiple Ukrainian oblasts, and a night-time Kinzhal missile attack was claimed.
key_points: Russia launched two Oreshik ballistic missiles on 24 May, one hitting Kyiv region and another falling in occupied Donetsk (Source: @UkraineNow/81496).

Ukraine's President Zelensky sent an urgent letter to the US highlighting a critical shortage of missile defence systems, relying almost exclusively on the United States (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30474).
Ukrainian defence minister announced a "logistical lockdown" program to scale strikes on Russian rear logistics, allocating 5 bn UAH for modern strike assets (Source: @UkraineNow/81493).

Multiple UAV sightings were reported: Sumy north towards Chernihiv, Sumy north towards Shostka, Kharkiv north towards Chuhuiv, Dnipropetrovsk towards Pavlohrad, and others (Source: @kpszsu/63040, @kpszsu/63059, @kpszsu/63055, @kpszsu/63037).
A night‑time Russian Kinzhal hypersonic missile attack was reported, with Ukrainian air defence claiming to have downed 147 UAVs (Source: @UkraineNow/81503).
Russian forces shelled a children's playground in Kherson with BM‑21 rockets, killing a father and wounding a mother and two young daughters (Source: @UkraineNow/81498).

Ukrainian intelligence noted ongoing occupation and fortification of Pokrovsk, Mirnograd, Kostyantynivka and pressure on Rodynske, with Russian drone crews controlling local airspace (Source: @DeepStateUA/23539).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched two Oreshik ballistic missiles on 24 May, striking Kyiv region and occupied Donetsk. Ukraine faces a critical shortage of missile defence, prompting Zelensky's urgent appeal to the United States. A logistical lockdown program has been initiated to intensify strikes on Russian rear logistics.

points: President Zelensky sent an urgent letter to the US warning of a critical shortage in missile defence, stating Ukraine relies almost exclusively on the United States for ballistic missile defence (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30474).

Defence Minister Fedir Fedorov announced a "logistical lockdown" program to scale middle‑strike attacks on Russian logistics, warehouses, and command posts, backed by an additional 5 bn UAH for modern strike systems (Source: @UkraineNow/81493).
Ukrainian forces reported a night‑time Kinzhal hypersonic missile attack, claiming air defence shot down 147 UAVs during the incident (Source: @UkraineNow/81503).

Ukrainian intelligence highlighted ongoing Russian occupation of Pokrovsk, Mirnograd and Kostyantynivka, with drone crews controlling local airspace and pressure on Rodynske, threatening further advances (Source: @DeepStateUA/23539).
A Russian BM‑21 rocket attack on a children's playground in Kherson killed a father and injured a mother and two young daughters, underscoring civilian vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/81498).
Russia launched two Oreshik ballistic missiles on 24 May; one struck Kyiv region, the other fell in occupied Donetsk, expanding the missile threat (Source: @UkraineNow/81496).

Russian tactical aviation activity was noted with AN‑124‑100 delivering possible H‑22/32 rockets to Dyagilevo, and AN‑72/IL‑76MD flights to Engels, Shatalovo, Balti‑more, and Kursk, suggesting transport of ballistic or cruise missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/93991).

Russian forces continued UAV operations across multiple fronts, including Sumy north towards Chernihiv, Kharkiv north towards Chuhuiv, and Dnipropetrovsk towards Pavlohrad (Source: @kpszsu/63059, @kpszsu/63055, @kpszsu/63037).
Russian troops intensified artillery fire on civilian infrastructure, exemplified by the BM‑21 strike on a Kherson playground (Source: @UkraineNow/81498).
Russian propaganda channels reported that attempts to block or capture Kaliningrad would have severe consequences for NATO, asserting Russia has all necessary resources (Source: @tass_agency/377890).

Ukrainian UAV reports indicate hostile drones moving from Sumy north towards Chernihiv and from Sumy north towards Shostka, suggesting pressure on the northeastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/63040, @kpszsu/63009).
Ukrainian sources noted a group of enemy UAVs on Sumy heading toward the regional centre, increasing aerial threat in the east (Source: @kpszsu/63028).
Russian tactical aviation activity was observed heading to eastern Russian airfields, possibly delivering missile payloads (Source: @eRadarrua/93991).
Ukrainian intelligence reported Russian drone crews establishing control over the skies of Pokrovsk and Mirnograd, complicating Ukrainian operations (Source: @DeepStateUA/23539).
Ukrainian forces reported a Kinzhal missile attack, which typically targets high‑value eastern locations, though specific impact area not detailed (Source: @UkraineNow/81503).
Ukrainian UAV sightings in northern Sumy heading toward Chernihiv indicate Russian aerial activity on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/63059).
Ukrainian reports of UAVs from the north of Kharkiv moving toward Chuhuiv suggest pressure on the northern Kharkiv axis (Source: @kpszsu/63055).
Russian tactical aviation flights to Engels and Shatalovo airfields in the north‑west may support operations on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93991).
Ukrainian intelligence noted increased Russian drone presence in the north‑eastern direction, threatening front‑line logistics (Source: @kpszsu/63044).
No specific ground engagements were detailed, but UAV activity implies heightened reconnaissance and strike capability in the north.
Russian BM‑21 rockets struck a children's playground in Kherson, causing civilian casualties and highlighting southern front aggression (Source: @UkraineNow/81498).
Ukrainian reports of UAVs from the Black Sea area heading toward Chornomorske/South indicate Russian maritime drone activity on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/63009).
Ukrainian logistical lockdown program aims to target Russian rear logistics, warehouses and command posts, many of which are situated in the southern operational depth (Source: @UkraineNow/81493).
Ukrainian forces reported a night‑time Kinzhal missile attack, which may have been directed at southern targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81503).
No explicit ground battles were reported, but aerial and missile threats remain high in the south.
Ukraine reported a night‑time Kinzhal hypersonic missile attack, with air defence claiming to have downed 147 UAVs (Source: @UkraineNow/81503).

Russian tactical aviation activity included AN‑124‑100 deliveries to Dyagilevo and AN‑72/IL‑76MD flights to Engels, Shatalovo, Balti‑more, and Kursk, likely transporting missile payloads (Source: @eRadarrua/93991).
Ukrainian UAV sightings across multiple oblasts (Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Poltava) indicate extensive Russian drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/63040‑63055).
Russian air defence reportedly engaged Ukrainian UAVs on the north‑eastern and eastern directions, indicating active air‑space contestation (Source: @kpszsu/63044).
Ukrainian air‑defence capabilities remain strained, with Zelensky's letter emphasizing reliance on US systems and a shortage of Patriot and PAC‑3 interceptors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30474).

key_developments: Russia launched two Oreshik ballistic missiles on 24 May, one striking Kyiv region and another falling in occupied Donetsk, expanding the missile threat (Source: @UkraineNow/81496).

President Zelensky sent an urgent letter to the United States warning of a critical shortage in missile defence, stating Ukraine relies almost exclusively on US systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30474).
Ukrainian defence minister announced a logistical lockdown program to intensify strikes on Russian rear logistics, backed by 5 bn UAH for modern strike assets (Source: @UkraineNow/81493).
A night‑time Russian Kinzhal hypersonic missile attack was reported, with Ukrainian air defence claiming to have downed 147 UAVs (Source: @UkraineNow/81503).
Multiple hostile UAV incursions were reported across Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk and other oblasts, indicating broad Russian drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/63040‑63055).
Russian BM‑21 rocket fire hit a children's playground in Kherson, killing a civilian and wounding a mother and two young daughters (Source: @UkraineNow/81498).

Ukrainian intelligence noted Russian occupation and fortification of Pokrovsk, Mirnograd, Kostyantynivka and pressure on Rodynske, with drone crews controlling local airspace (Source: @DeepStateUA/23539).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and drone activity remains high, with ballistic missile strikes reaching deep into Ukrainian territory and extensive UAV incursions across multiple fronts. Ukraine's air defence shortages, highlighted by Zelensky's appeal, limit its ability to counter these threats, while the Kinzhal attack demonstrates Russia's willingness to employ hypersonic weapons. The logistical lockdown program may increase pressure on Russian rear areas but could also provoke intensified retaliatory strikes.
sections.regional_response: Western partners, particularly the United States, are being urged to accelerate delivery of Patriot and PAC‑3 systems to address Ukraine's air defence gaps. EU officials have signaled concerns over Russian aggression, though no concrete new aid was detailed in the messages. Russia continues to assert its capability to counter NATO actions, especially regarding Kaliningrad, indicating a broader strategic posture.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV sightings provide actionable data on Russian reconnaissance routes, especially north‑south corridors in Sumy and Kharkiv. The logistical lockdown initiative suggests upcoming Ukrainian strikes on Russian supply lines, warranting monitoring of transport hubs like Dyagilevo and Engels. Civilian casualty reports, such as the Kherson playground attack, highlight the need for reinforced protection of populated areas.
